skill.name: slack
skill.description: Automate Slack workspace navigation, search, message review, screenshots, and structured extraction through an authenticated browser session.
skill.summary: Use browser automation to inspect Slack channels, DMs, unreads, search results, reactions, pins, and conversation context.
skill.author: vercel-labs

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution findings are mostly true positives because the skill documents Bash-based agent-browser automation, but the commands are for the declared Slack browser workflow rather than hidden malware. The high-severity weak-crypto, screen-capture upload, scheduling, and reconnaissance patterns are false positives in prose, templates, or benign screenshots; the main confirmed risk is exposure or alteration of private Slack workspace data. No prompt injection attempt was found in the scanned files.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Slack Data Exposure
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The skill directs agents to use an authenticated Slack browser session to search conversations, extract message content, collect usernames, timestamps, reactions, and capture screenshots. This is legitimate for a Slack automation skill, but it can expose private workspace data if used without authorization or data-minimization controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files explicitly describe extracting Slack message content and saving screenshots or JSON snapshots. The risk is privacy exposure, not hidden exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: External Browser Automation Commands
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The static external command findings correspond to Bash examples that invoke agent-browser and npx agent-browser. The commands are expected for this skill, but they can click Slack UI controls, search private content, and perform actions in the active user session.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are direct shell commands, but they are limited to the declared browser automation tool and documented Slack workflow. No arbitrary dynamic shell construction was found.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Local Artifacts
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The skill recommends saving Slack snapshots, screenshots, and reports to local files. These artifacts may contain message text, channel names, user names, timestamps, and private workspace context.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation clearly instructs the creation of screenshots and JSON snapshots from Slack. This is useful but creates durable copies of sensitive communications.

low_findings.title: Benign Slack URLs
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The hardcoded URLs point to Slack application or help pages, and one reference is an example Slack archive URL used to explain timestamp structure. No evidence found of data being sent to an unrelated endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are Slack-owned or Slack workspace examples and align with the declared purpose. No suspicious external collection service appears in the scanned files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern Is Prose
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The static weak-cryptography alerts occur in markdown prose, examples, or template text rather than cryptographic code. No MD5, SHA-1, or similar weak hashing implementation was found in the scanned documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed lines are natural language or report placeholders, not executable crypto usage. The repository contains markdown instructions, not cryptographic functions.

low_findings.title: Other Blocker Patterns Are Contextual False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The screen capture, at scheduling, and system reconnaissance alerts map to ordinary Slack screenshot guidance, timestamp prose, and UI inspection instructions. No evidence found of screenshot upload, job scheduling, or host reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content describes screenshots, Slack timestamps, and browser page state checks. It does not instruct uploading captures or inspecting the local operating system.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash-Controlled Browser Automation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on Bash commands that control a browser connected to Slack. This pattern should require user authorization before reading, clicking, searching, or posting in an authenticated workspace.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command blocks are explicit and repeated. The risk depends on the active Slack session and user permissions.

dangerous_patterns.title: Slack Content Written to Files
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented workflow writes screenshots and JSON snapshots that may include Slack content. Users should redact or delete local artifacts when they include private messages.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The files explicitly show redirects to JSON and screenshot outputs. The sensitivity comes from Slack data captured in those files.

content.value_statement: Slack workspaces can hide important updates across channels, DMs, threads, and search results. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through browser-based Slack review and reporting.
actual_capabilities: Connects to an existing browser session or opens Slack in a browser.
Uses accessibility snapshots to identify Slack channels, DMs, tabs, and controls.
Checks unread messages across Activity, DMs, and channel lists.
Searches Slack conversations and captures result context.
Creates screenshots and JSON snapshots for reports or later review.
Provides a report template for unread counts, channels, conversations, and evidence.
limitations: Requires an authenticated Slack browser session with the right workspace access.
Uses browser automation, not the Slack API or bot tokens.
Element references change between sessions and require fresh snapshots.
Can capture sensitive Slack data, so users must review privacy and retention rules.

use_cases.title: Review Team Unreads
use_cases.description: Check unread channels and DMs before planning follow-up messages or meetings.

use_cases.title: Investigate Conversation History
use_cases.description: Search channels for incidents, decisions, owners, timestamps, and related thread context.

use_cases.title: Prepare Workspace Reports
use_cases.description: Collect screenshots, channel summaries, and action items into a structured Slack analysis report.

prompt_templates.title: Check Unread Slack Items
prompt_templates.prompt: Check my Slack unreads. Summarize unread DMs, unread channels, and any urgent items. Ask before opening private conversations.

prompt_templates.title: Search for a Topic
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Slack for messages about [topic]. Return channels, senders, dates, and a short summary. Avoid storing raw private content unless needed.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Channel Activity Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Review #[channel] activity for [date range]. Summarize major discussions, decisions, open questions, and action items with screenshot evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Cross-Channel Context
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced investigation across multiple Slack areas
prompt_templates.prompt: Investigate [incident or project] across relevant Slack channels and threads. Build a concise report with timeline, participants, evidence, and privacy redactions.

output_examples.output: Activity shows three unread items. Two are in project channels and one is a direct message. The project items need review today.

output_examples.input: Search Slack for deployment errors from this week.
output_examples.output: The search found matching discussions in engineering and incidents. Most messages refer to one failed rollout, with follow-up owners assigned.

output_examples.input: Prepare a report on the product-design channel.
output_examples.output: The report summarizes recent topics, active participants, pinned items, unresolved questions, and screenshot references for verification.

best_practices: Get user approval before opening private DMs, exporting snapshots, or sending messages.
Take a fresh snapshot before every click because Slack element references change.
Redact screenshots and reports that include private messages, user names, or customer data.
anti_patterns: Do not automate Slack actions from another user account or unauthorized workspace.
Do not save raw conversation exports when a short summary is enough.
Do not rely on stale element references from a previous browser session.

faq.question: Does this skill use the Slack API?
faq.answer: No. It uses browser automation against a Slack web session that the user can access.

faq.question: Can it send Slack messages?
faq.answer: It can guide browser actions that may send messages, but users should approve any posting action first.

faq.question: Does it require Slack admin permissions?
faq.answer: No. It only sees what the authenticated browser session can already see.

faq.question: Can it read private channels or DMs?
faq.answer: Only if the active Slack account already has access. Treat all captured content as sensitive.

faq.question: What evidence can it collect?
faq.answer: It can collect screenshots, accessibility snapshots, visible message details, channel names, timestamps, reactions, and report notes.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning. The main concerns are user authorization, privacy, and local retention of Slack artifacts.
